records.title: Hospital and medical-specialist care
records.description: Medical-specialist care is generally delivered in hospitals or specialist practices, usually requires a valid referral and is covered under policy and effectiveness conditions in the basic package.
records.ai_summary: Confirm the referral, insurer contract and any prior authorisation before the appointment. Specialist treatment and follow-up commonly use the compulsory deductible even when the referral comes from a GP.

records.title: Referral to a medical specialist
records.description: Most specialist hospital care needs a medically justified referral from an authorised professional, and treatment without a valid referral may not be accepted or reimbursed.

official_source.title: Wanneer heb ik een verwijsbrief van de huisarts nodig?

records.ai_summary: Ask the referrer which provider and specialty are suitable, then verify referral validity and policy conditions before booking. A referral does not by itself guarantee full reimbursement.

records.title: Hospital admission and insured stay
records.description: A medically necessary hospital stay connected with covered specialist treatment can include nursing, related medicines and other care, subject to statutory duration and deductible rules.
records.ai_summary: Ask what the admission covers, which specialist is responsible and what discharge support is planned. Hospital stay under the Zvw normally counts toward the compulsory deductible.

records.title: Patient rights during medical treatment
records.description: Patients have rights to clear information, shared decisions, refusal of treatment, privacy, record access and a second opinion, alongside duties to provide relevant information and pay lawful costs.

official_source.title: Wat zijn mijn rechten en plichten bij een medische behandeling?

records.ai_summary: Ask for understandable benefits, risks and alternatives before consenting. Patients may refuse care, review their records and request another medical opinion while continuing to communicate relevant facts to the care team.

records.title: Access and correct medical records
records.description: Patients can request access and copies of their medical records, ask to correct factual errors, add their own statement and in some circumstances request early deletion of information.

official_source.title: Accessing, updating or deleting medical records

records.ai_summary: Request records from the responsible provider or patient portal and review reports, results and referral letters. Ask for factual corrections in writing and distinguish correction from adding a personal disagreement statement.

records.title: Hospital waiting lists and care mediation
records.description: Providers must tell patients about insurer care mediation when a wait exceeds the applicable norm or when a patient stop prevents timely access to treatment.
records.ai_summary: Ask the provider for the expected intake and treatment dates and whether they exceed the norm. If so, request written information and contact the insurer for a faster suitable provider.

records.title: Understand DBC hospital billing
records.description: Dutch hospitals group specialist activities into diagnosis-treatment combination care products, which determine the claim sent to the insurer rather than billing every individual action separately.
records.ai_summary: Review hospital claims in the insurer portal and compare the treatment period and care product with the care received. Ask the provider or insurer to explain unexpected items before disputing a bill.

records.title: Complain about a healthcare provider
records.description: A patient should normally raise a concern with the provider, may use the free complaints officer and can then approach the provider's recognised dispute body if the complaint remains unresolved.
records.ai_summary: Write down what happened, when, who was involved and the desired resolution. Start with the provider or complaints officer and preserve the response before escalating to a dispute body or regulator.

records.title: Report a care-quality signal to the inspectorate
records.description: Patients and relatives can share care-quality concerns with the national healthcare inspectorate's reporting point, which records signals for supervision but does not itself resolve the private complaint.
records.ai_summary: Use the provider complaint route to seek a personal solution and the IGJ reporting point to share a quality or safety signal. The inspectorate uses reports for oversight rather than awarding compensation.

records.title: Medical decisions when a patient lacks capacity
records.description: When a patient cannot make a treatment decision, clinicians check any advance directive and appointed representative before following the statutory order for a partner or close relative to represent the patient.

official_source.title: Wie beslist er over mijn medische behandeling als ik dit zelf niet (meer) kan?

records.ai_summary: Discuss an advance directive and representative with the doctor while decision-making capacity is intact. Capacity is assessed for the specific decision, and the representative should follow the patient's wishes and interests.
